title: American Express Australia Limited — Consumer harm
investigation_objective: The Federal Court found American Express Australia Limited breached design and distribution obligations for two co-branded credit cards distributed through David Jones stores between 25 May and 5 July 2022. Amex ought to have known that high cancelled-application rates suggested the target market determinations were no longer appropriate but continued issuing the cards without reviewing them. Amex admitted the contraventions and was ordered to pay $8 million plus ASIC's costs, described as the first quantified penalty imposed under Australia's design and distribution obligations regime.
scope: Admitted breaching design and distribution obligations for two credit card products; Ordered to pay an $8 million penalty, described as the first quantified penalty under the DDO regime; Regulator found the company continued issuing a product despite signs its target market determination was no longer appropriate
